All  objectives, identified in the  beginning, were achieved:  The  generations concept was identified and the theories of generations were discussed; The influence of  the theories of  generation s  on organizations’ strategic management was analyzed ;   Previous generational shifts and their impact on businesses were  discussed ;  G lobal trends related to  Generation Z  and  their  impact on the markets  was considered ; P eculiarities of Generation Z in the CIS  countries  and their impact on the markets  were discussed ;  An a nalysis on how companies have adapted to generations shifts from the historical perspective and how they are currently adapting to Generation Z peculiarities  was conducted ; P roposals f or  international  companies  willing to  adapt to peculiarities of Generation Z  in the CIS market  were formulated. Analysis of global trends and theories associated with Generation Z was conducted with a help of secondary data, and a survey and multiple interviews with international companies’ managers were conducted for identifying peculiarities of zoomers in the CIS market. As the research was being conducted, drastic differences between the patterns of behavior on consumer and labor markets of Generation Z and their predecessors millennials were identified. In terms of comparing results of surveys on Generation Z from a global perspective and local CIS peculiarities, there were also many similarities and differences, all of them could affect the perception of a company by zoomer-stakeholders.  The key  identified  similarities  in patterns of behavior of Generation Z globally and particularly in the CIS region included: Similar preference for individual work instead of team tasks; An ability to maintain a work-life balance as a top priority when choosing a job; Strong concern for own mental health; A need for constant supervisor support and detailed guidelines at every step of a working-process; A belief that organizations’ main goal is to improve the welfare of a society; Unwillingness to purchase products of brands involved in controversial advertising with any kinds of discrimination, including racism, sexism, homophobia, and etc. The differences  between the results  of a survey among Generation Z from the CIS region and the results  of global surveys  include the fact that zoomers from the countries of CIS seem to be less concerned about sustainable consumption, are likely to gain loyalty towards producers,  are less criticizing of companies using immoral advertising, and have different priorities when looking for a job. The discrepancies between results of discussed surveys and the one conducted specifically for this research may be due to different reasons, including socioeconomic factors, discussed in the paper.  Interviews with HR and marketing managers from multiple international companies showed that all of those companies are aware of and concerned by intergenerational differences, but most are currently starting to adapt to Generation Z, as its representatives have just recently become solvent stakeholders. To help businesses adapt, an  analysis of  Generation Z as consumers, job seekers and employees was conducted, and proposals were formulated for companies’ corporate strategies and business-processes: Use sharing-friendly platforms  Translate a message that resonates with the audience  Offer communities  Associate your bran with socially important causes  Create meaningful and/or snappy content  Build a community working for a greater cause Provide instant gratification and continuous supervisor support  Respect employees’ work-life balance and personal boundaries The strategic proposals are based on Generation Z’s patterns of behavior in consuming and working, on their preferences, values, and expectations from brands and employers. The proposals are aimed to help international companies present in the CIS region adapt to the peculiarities of the new wave of consumers and employees , and they take into account both global practices and experiences, and local CIS specificities.
